source.title: Trajectory encoding in the hippocampus and entorhinal cortex

confidence_rationale: Two-level coding result: CA1 disambiguates by trajectory context, deep-layer EC generalises across trajectories at shared positions. Argues for multi-granularity anchor substrate rather than single uniform anchor level. Direct empirical support for (place, context) anchors plus a parallel pure-place generalisation pathway.
failure_signatures: If the substrate uses only schema-region-level anchors, it loses the EC-deep-layer generalisation pathway that supports transfer across trajectories sharing a common location.
Routing at the (place, context) level only -- if the downstream readout to E2/E3 does not pool across context-disambiguated anchors at the place level, generalisation will fail.

mapping.source_claim_statement: CA1 cells fire differentially at the same physical position depending on trajectory context (prospective or retrospective coding). Deep-layer EC cells generalise across trajectory differences and represent shared spatial features.
mapping.ree_translation: Two-level architecture: (place, context) anchors at the disambiguation level and pure-place pooling at the integration/generalisation level. The substrate plan should support both levels rather than collapse to one granularity. MECH-272 routing operates at the CA1 disambiguation level; downstream readout to E2/E3 should pool at the place level.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The exact aspect of trajectory being encoded (prospective vs retrospective vs sequence position) is not dissected. The EC generalisation result is from deep layers only. Mapping the two-level architecture to specific REE consumers requires interpretation.
mapping.source_context: Single-unit recordings from CA1 and entorhinal cortex of rats navigating multiple distinct trajectories with shared positions.

confidence_components.notes: Foundational paper, strong source quality. Mapping fidelity moderate -- the architectural translation supports the multi-granularity claim but the specific mapping to anchor and consumer levels in the substrate is interpretive.
